tells part of the story. What's more significant is ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"what ByteDance is doing with these chips."}],""]}],["$","h3","44",{"style":{"fontSize":"24px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"36px","marginBottom":"16px","color":"#22d3ee"},"children":"From Inference to Training"}],["$","p","46",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["Initially, Chinese companies used domestic chips primarily for inference—running already-trained models. Training remained the domain of Nvidia hardware, with Chinese labs stockpiling H100s and H800s before sanctions tightened."]}],["$","p","48",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["ByteDance's deal signals a strategic shift. According to sources familiar with the agreement, ByteDance plans to use Ascend chips for ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"both training and inference workloads"}],", with joint R&D labs established to optimize Doubao's training frameworks for Huawei's architecture."]}],["$","blockquote","50",{"style":{"borderLeft":"3px solid #22d3ee","paddingLeft":"20px","margin":"20px 0","color":"#a3a3a3","fontStyle":"italic"},"children":"**Technical Context**: The Ascend 910 delivers approximately 256 TFLOPS of FP16 performance—comparable to Nvidia's V100 but roughly 32% behind the H200. However, Huawei's MindSpore framework has closed significant ground through software optimization, with training efficiency reportedly improving 40% year-over-year."}],["$","p","52",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["This matters because training is where AI capabilities are born. If Chinese companies can train competitive models on domestic hardware, the strategic calculus of chip sanctions fundamentally changes."]}],["$","p","54",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["---"]}],["$","h2","56",{"style":{"fontSize":"30px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"48px","marginBottom":"24px","color":"#e5e5e5","borderBottom":"1px solid #1a1a1a","paddingBottom":"12px"},"children":"Chapter 2: DeepSeek V4 and the Sovereignty Test"}],["$","h3","58",{"style":{"fontSize":"24px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"36px","marginBottom":"16px","color":"#22d3ee"},"children":"The Most Watched Launch in AI"}],["$","p","60",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["Perhaps no upcoming release is more consequential than ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"DeepSeek V4"}],". The Hangzhou-based startup, which shocked markets in January 2025 with its $5.6 million training cost for R1, is preparing to unveil its next-generation model—and the industry is watching closely for one specific detail."]}],["$","h4","62",{"style":{"fontSize":"18px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"24px","marginBottom":"12px","color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"What chips trained it?"}],["$","p","64",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["According to The Information and multiple industry sources, DeepSeek V4 is being trained on a hybrid infrastructure combining ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"Huawei Ascend 910Cs with previously stockpiled Nvidia hardware"}],". The exact ratio is a closely guarded secret, but analysts estimate domestic chips now constitute 60-70% of DeepSeek's training compute."]}],["$","div","71",{"style":{"overflowX":"auto","margin":"24px questions:"]}],["$","ol","81",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","80",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Can domestic If V4 matches or exceeds GPT-4o performance while running primarily on Ascend hardware, it proves Chinese silicon is viable for frontier AI development."}]]}],["$","ol","83",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","82",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"What's the cost differential? Can Chinese labs absorb this premium?"}]]}],["$","ol","85",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","84",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Will others follow? It's a political statement. If they can prove you can train GPT-4 level models on Chinese chips, the entire sanctions strategy looks questionable.\" — Anonymous chip industry executive"}],["$","p","88",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["---"]}],["$","h2","90",{"style":{"fontSize":"30px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"48px","marginBottom":"24px","color":"#e5e5e5","borderBottom":"1px solid #1a1a1a","paddingBottom":"12px"},"children":"Chapter 3: The Nvidia H200 Wildcard"}],["$","h3","92",{"style":{"fontSize":"24px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"36px","marginBottom":"16px","color":"#22d3ee"},"children":"Trump's Surprising Move"}],["$","p","94",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["In a twist that surprised many industry observers, the Trump administration approved the sale of ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"over 400,000 Nvidia H200 chips"}]," to Chinese tech giants in early 2025. The deal, reportedly negotiated during a visit by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, allows ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent to each purchase approximately 200,000 units."]}],["$","h4","96",{"style":{"fontSize":"18px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"24px","marginBottom":"12px","color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"But there's a It's about ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"who defines the future of artificial intelligence"}],"—and by extension, the future of economic and military power."]}],["$","p","302",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["American strategy assumed that controlling advanced semiconductor exports would maintain technological leadership. The emerging reality suggests otherwise:"]}],["$","ol","305",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","304",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Innovation under constraint: Chinese labs have developed novel optimization techniques, model architectures, and training methodologies specifically designed for constrained compute environments. Some of these innovations may prove applicable globally."}]]}],["$","ol","307",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","306",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Ecosystem resilience: The forced development of a parallel software stack means China now has full vertical integration—from chips to frameworks to applications. This independence is strategically valuable regardless of performance gaps."}]]}],["$","ol","309",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","308",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Cost disruption: Even if Chinese chips remain 20-30% less efficient, the 50-70% cost advantage creates compelling economics for price-sensitive global markets."}]]}],["$","ol","311",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","310",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"Talent rebalancing: The requirement to work with domestic stacks has created hundreds of thousands of engineers skilled in non-CUDA architectures—a workforce that will drive continued innovation."}]]}],["$","blockquote","312",{"style":{"borderLeft":"3px solid #22d3ee","paddingLeft":"20px","margin":"20px 0","color":"#a3a3a3","fontStyle":"italic"},"children":"**Analyst Quote**: \"The question isn't whether China can catch up—it's whether the West can maintain its lead while fighting on a battlefield where its opponent sets 50% of the rules.\" — Georgetown AI policy researcher"}],["$","p","314",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["---"]}],["$","h2","316",{"style":{"fontSize":"30px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"48px","marginBottom":"24px","color":"#e5e5e5","borderBottom":"1px solid #1a1a1a","paddingBottom":"12px"},"children":"Conclusion: The Wall Becomes a Door"}],["$","p","318",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["Fifteen months after the most aggressive chip sanctions in history, China's AI industry hasn't collapsed. It has transformed."]}],["$","p","320",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["ByteDance's $5.6 billion Huawei deal. DeepSeek's imminent V4 launch on domestic silicon. The MindSpore ecosystem's rapid maturation. These aren't acts of desperation—they're the foundations of a genuinely independent AI capability."]}],["$","p","322",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["The \"Great Silicon Wall\" America tried to build hasn't stopped Chinese AI progress. Instead, it has forced the construction of a ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"parallel infrastructure"}]," that may eventually compete on equal terms—and in some dimensions, superior economics."]}],["$","p","324",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["For global technology leaders, the message is clear: ",["$","strong","1",{"style":{"color":"#e5e5e5"},"children":"The unipolar AI era is ending."}]," The next decade will be defined by competition between two technological civilizations, each with distinct advantages and limitations."]}],["$","p","326",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["The chip war was supposed to slow China down. Instead, it may have accelerated the arrival of a multipolar AI world."]}],["$","p","328",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["---"]}],["$","h2","330",{"style":{"fontSize":"30px","fontWeight":600,"marginTop":"48px","marginBottom":"24px","color":"#e5e5e5","borderBottom":"1px solid #1a1a1a","paddingBottom":"12px"},"children":"Related Articles"}],["$","ul","336",{"style":{"margin":"20px 0","paddingLeft":"24px","color":"#d4d4d4"},"children":[["$","li","332",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"[China's AI Model Wars: Alibaba, ByteDance, and MiniMax Reshape Global Competition](/blog/china-ai-model-wars-april-2026)"}],["$","li","333",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"[DeepSeek-V3: The $5.6M Training Run That Shocked Silicon Valley](/blog/deepseek-v3-deep-dive)"}],["$","li","334",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"[Doubao's 12 Trillion Token Explosion: ByteDance's Quiet Victory](/blog/doubao-12-trillion-token-explosion)"}],["$","li","335",{"style":{"margin":"10px 0","lineHeight":1.7},"children":"[ModelBest Becomes Unicorn: Tsinghua's Edge AI Pioneer](/blog/modelbest-edge-ai-unicorn-2026)"}]]}],["$","p","337",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["---"]}],["$","p","339",{"style":{"fontSize":"17px","lineHeight":1.8,"color":"#d4d4d4","margin":"16px 0"},"children":["*Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only.
